description: Roundabout-entry lateral crossing: ego approaches the roundabout conflict point while a truck enters from the right/front-right on a constructed collision course. The nominal leftward T-drift escape for a right-side threat is constrained by a pedestrian on the left escape side, and a follower behind the ego makes hard braking nontrivial. Sweep actionable TTC, crossing speed, ego speed, and pedestrian-side escape gap to expose brake-vs-drift tradeoffs outside current normal-road rules.
hypothesis: With a pedestrian on the left escape side, right-side-crossing T-drift should dominate only at short TTC/high crossing speed when the pedestrian gap is large; otherwise controlled braking should dominate, especially at longer TTC or small escape gaps.
